# Get started with Copilot Service workspace

Copilot Service workspace helps customer service representatives (service representatives or representatives) increase productivity with a browser-like, tabbed experience. Service representatives can use the app to work on multiple cases and conversations. It's a modern, customizable, high-productivity application that allows them to work on multiple sessions at a time in a single workspace.

The application uses artificial intelligence in productivity tools like Smart Assist to identify similar cases and relevant articles, thereby boosting representative productivity. Features such as scripts and macros provide representatives with guidance and resources to automate repetitive tasks to achieve a great customer experience.

Learn more about licensing and system requirements in [Copilot Service workspace system requirements](customer-service-workspace-system-requirements).

Set up Omnichannel in Copilot Service workspace: With the Dynamics 365 Customer Service Digital Messaging add-on, the representative who work on cases can also engage with customers via channels like Live Chat, voice, and SMS without leaving the Copilot Service workspace app.

[Screenshot of the enhanced multisession Copilot Service workspace]

## Copilot Service workspace sessions and tabs
Copilot Service workspace allows representatives to work on multiple sessions at a time in a single app while keeping the work organized. Representatives can work on up to nine sessions and within a session, they can open up to 10 tabs. A new session starts when a representative opens a case from the Home session or accepts an incoming conversation. If the representative opens the customer record from a session, a new tab opens in the same session. Representatives can select the hamburger icon to access the site map. When a representative opens a page from the site map, the page loads in the current focused session.

### Navigate sessions and tabs table:
Open a record from Home session -> new session. Open a record from Global search -> new session. Open a record from retrieved search records -> focused session. Open a record using Quick Create notification -> new session. Create a new record -> new session. Open a record from timeline -> new tab in focused session. Open a record from a form lookup -> new tab in focused session. Open a view from site map -> new tab in focused session. Open a dashboard from site map -> new tab in focused session. Expand Recent in site map -> records/views opened from anchor tab only.

### Session restore (preview)
If your administrator turned on session restore, key entities in Copilot Service workspace reload automatically if your browser refreshes. Without session restore, when a browser refreshes or reconnects, Copilot Service workspace reloads only the home page. Session restore automatically restores cases, accounts, and their associated application tabs without requiring manual reopening. Focus is restored to the last session or application tab in view. After the presence is loaded, conversations, including calls and chats, are reinstated. Learn more in Enable session restore to automatically reopen sessions and tabs (preview).

### Use the Inbox
If administrator turned on inbox, representatives see cases/conversations/activities assigned to them; used for high-velocity tasks; inbox sessions can be promoted to regular sessions. Asynchronous channels in conversation inbox: SMS, persistent chat, Facebook, WeChat, LINE, WhatsApp, Microsoft Teams. Learn more in Configure the inbox view.

### Use the productivity pane with Smart Assist
Productivity pane on right side displays intelligence-driven suggestions using Smart Assist to suggest related cases/knowledge articles; scripts guide through steps with potentially automated actions via macros. Learn more in Productivity pane.

## Work with cases, activities, knowledge articles, and email templates
On the Customer Service Representative Dashboard: view cases/activities assigned to you, view cases available to work on, create/delete/filter cases and activities from workspace.

### Customize the representative experience with Copilot Service admin center
Experience profiles enable targeted app experiences for representatives and supervisors (alternative to custom apps) - session templates, conversation channels, productivity tools assigned to users. Learn more in Experience profiles.

## Considerations
Recommend single browser instance. XRM.Navigation.openForm/navigateTo APIs behave like multisession app (new session from Home; new tab in focused session from a case session). Xrm.Navigation.openWebResource opens new browser window (doesn't remove nav/command bars); Microsoft.Apm.createTab opens web resources as session tabs; sessions/tabs can be opened via Microsoft.Apm APIs. Multisession only supported in Copilot Service workspace app, not custom apps/copies.

### Limitations
Switching tabs/sessions: subgrid controls don't retain filter/sort conditions; web resources/form components/custom pages/third-party sites don't retain page state (form components may retain unsaved text field values but not lookup fields); status record changes in one tab don't auto-refresh corresponding record open in a grid/subgrid of another tab. Not supported on mobile devices, Unified Service Desk, Microsoft Teams, or Dynamics 365 Customer Engagement (on-premises).

## Deprecated Customer Service workspace layout
Legacy layout deprecated, removed October 2023.

### Turn off the close session dialog
F12 dev tools console: Xrm.Utility.getGlobalContext().saveSettingValue("msdyn_SuppressSessionCloseWarning",true); refresh app page.

### Training resources
Download Copilot Service workspace in a day training (fwlink linkid=2241180): sessions/tabs/productivity tools, experience profiles/templates/scripts/macros, security-role assignment, moving profiles via solutions, multisession APIs (Microsoft.Apm). Migrate from Unified Service Desk to Copilot Service workspace playbook. Related information: Work with cases in Customer Service Hub, Overview of the productivity pane, View the agent calendar.

# Manage Copilot features in Customer Service

Copilot in Customer Service provides real-time AI assistance: representatives respond to questions, compose emails, draft chat responses, summarize cases/conversations in Copilot Service workspace app. For Customer Service Hub / custom apps, admin must perform steps in Enable Copilot features (copilot-powerapps-settings#enable-copilot-features-for-custom-apps). Note: Summarize conversations available in Copilot Service workspace only.

## How Copilot features are delivered
Powered by Microsoft 365 Copilot, exposed in Customer Service apps such as Copilot Service workspace, using case/customer/interaction data to retrieve information, generate responses, complete actions without leaving workflow. Some capabilities delivered via Copilot agents such as Service Agent, extending Copilot with case summarization/knowledge retrieval/actions. Managed via Copilot Service admin center.

## Prerequisites
System Administrator role required. Licensing requirements apply (Dynamics 365 Licensing Guide). Copilot features enabled for Customer Service in Power Platform admin center.

### Enable Copilot features
1. Power Platform admin center site map -> Copilot. 2. Settings > Copilot under Dynamics 365 Customer Service. 3. Search environment, select, Edit setting. 4. Select Enable AI-powered Copilot features checkbox, save.

## Region availability and data movement
Copilot AI features available "in region" by default for environments with US, Australia, India, UK, government cloud regions (GCC, GCC High) as geography. Europe: data movement enabled by default. Other regions: enable data movement across regions in Power Platform admin center before opting in. Respond-to-questions/compose-email/summarize-cases-and-conversations features GA in North America region only; preview in rest of supported regions.

### Understand cross region data movement for Copilot features
Table: Azure OpenAI capacity in tenant region -> no data movement needed. Capacity outside tenant -> data movement required (Yes). Capacity in tenant region with fallback to external -> optional (throttling/unavailability risk if not enabled). Data movement across regions enabled by default in Power Platform admin center.

## Supported languages
See Language support for AI-based analytics and insights in Customer Service.

## Data sharing for Copilot features
Data sharing toggle in Power Platform admin center allows Microsoft to capture/manually review customer data (natural language inputs/outputs/telemetry) to build/improve/validate features/services/ML models; not used to train Azure OpenAI Service foundation models.

## Opt in to continue with Copilot setup
Copilot Service admin center Copilot settings / Summaries pages show: error message to allow data movement (if unavailable region or opting out), link to Power Platform admin center for data movement, data movement status, link to terms with Opt in option.

## Opt out of Copilot features
Opt out via Copilot settings or Summaries page (Support experience > Productivity > Copilot settings; Operations > Insights > Summaries). Opting out erases training data; re-opt-in required to use again.

## Make Copilot available to representatives
Enable Copilot features in Add users to experience profiles; out-of-the-box experience profile representatives can use Copilot by default. Custom experience profile can restrict features, assigned to representatives. For Customer Service Hub, enabling the feature makes it available to all representatives.

## Configure Copilot features for representatives
1. Copilot Service admin center, Support experience > Productivity. 2. Copilot settings > Manage. 3. Select checkboxes: Copilot immersive, Copilot side pane.

### Configure Customer Support agent settings
Copilot Service admin center > Agents within Copilot > Settings next to Customer Support. Configure across tabs: Overview, Immersive settings, Email settings, Prompts, Extend agent.

## Configure Admin Management agent settings
Admin Management (preview) agent: centralized management of admin prompts, user access, tools/knowledge via Copilot Studio. Copilot Service admin center > Agents within Copilot > Settings next to Admin Management. Configure across tabs: Overview, Prompts, User access. Manage tools/knowledge via Copilot Studio links on Overview tab.

## Let representatives use Copilot to translate responses
Copilot settings page > Translation using Copilot > Let representatives translate responses using Copilot; representatives select from configured Dynamics 365 languages.

## Record representative interactions with Copilot
Copilot settings page > Support experience data section > Record transcripts of representative interactions with Copilot (usage patterns/improve performance); can download and analyze knowledge sources/build usage reports.

## Assign roles and privileges
Customer Service Representative role can use Copilot features out of the box. Custom roles need privileges table: Copilot event (msdyn_copilotevent) Create/Read/Write Yes; Copilot interaction (msdyn_copilotinteraction) Create/Read Yes, Append Yes; Copilot interaction data (msdyn_copilotinteractiondata) Create/Read/Write Yes, Append To Yes; Agent Preference For Copilot (msdyn_copilotagentpreference) Create/Read/Write Yes; AI Model (msdyn_aimodel) Read Yes; AI Template (msdyn_aitemplate) Read Yes; ConversationInsight (msdyn_conversationinsight) Read Yes; Copilot Transcript Data (msdyn_copilottranscriptdata) Create/Read/Write/Append Yes; App profile (msdyn_appconfiguration) Read Yes; Pane tool configuration (msdyn_panetoolconfiguration) Read Yes; Productivity pane configuration (msdyn_paneconfiguration) Read Yes; Pane tab configuration (msdyn_panetabconfiguration) Read Yes; Organization Setting (organizationsetting) Read Yes.
Notes: users need Miscellaneous privileges > prvIntelligenceUsage assigned to required custom security roles to access Copilot. Custom roles for representatives need Read/Write at Basic access level for prvReadmsdyn_copilotevent/prvWritemsdyn_copilotevent, Create at Global level for prvCreatemsdyn_copilotevent. Custom CSR Manager roles need all permissions (Read/Create/Write) at Global access level.
